posture: Quiet Monday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, AI workforce readiness, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the strongest recent 'adopt' material remains Friday's watchlist cluster (Wollongong's Canva Goals world-first, the MAV/ALGA National AI Working Group and the three-council Adelaide AI partnership) logged 18 July.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the free §1a–§1d sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Recurring or out-of-window items were reviewed and excluded: the Hawkesbury/ARC Ento Tech black-soldier-fly waste-to-resource trial is a January 2026 development, not a fresh dated event today; Council Magazine's 'AI in local government shifting from pilot to practice' is April 2026 background; the federal Office of AI and Australian AI Standards (announced 15 July) sit with the Commonwealth rather than a council function and are out of window; and the UK MHCLG 'Extract' AI-planning rollout (June) and Bloomberg Harvard City Leadership Initiative tenth-class intake were prior-period or policy-network background. Already-logged material from 18 July (Wollongong Canva Goals, MAV/ALGA National AI Working Group, the Charles Sturt–Marion–Port Adelaide Enfield shared AI procurement, Scenic Rim nature-based flood works, Staffordshire AI business case) was not repeated. Paid titles (AFR/SMH/LGC/MJ) were held to headline + standfirst and not used; LGC's 15 July waste-reform piece was out of window regardless. Today is Monday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. Sections are published empty per §6.
